content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 16, 1992

Veronica Jamba
Los Angeles County Superior Court
111 North Hill Street, Room 105E
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Assistance
Our File No. I-92-387

Dear Ms. Jamba:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance provided to you concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Since you do not identify the person or persons to whom this question applies, we treat your request as one for informal assistance under Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A) and (C).2/

You asked whether pro tem judges are required to file statements of economic interests (Form 721) under the provisions of the Political Reform Act. Regulation 18724 provides:

Court commissioners, pro tem or retired judges shall not file statements of economic interests pursuant to Government Code Sections 87200, et seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than 30 days in any 12 month period. Provided, however, that this section shall not excuse these individuals from any filing obligations imposed by a conflict of interest code adopted pursuant to Government Code Sections 87300, et seq.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (83114; regulation 18329 (c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Veronica Jamba
Our File No. G-92-387
Page 2


Regulation 18724 is applicable to judges who are assigned to
hear cases on behalf of the County in its duty to provide judges
to hear such cases. Judges who hear cases under a private
agreement to which the County is not a party are not covered by
Regulation 18724.

I hope this letter satisfactorily responds to your question.
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have questions concerning
this letter.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

sections.question: You asked whether pro tern judges are required to file statements of economic interests (Form 721) under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: Regulation 18724 provides: Court commissioners, pro tern or retired judges shall not file statements of economic interests pursuant to Government Code Sections 87200, seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than 30 days in any 12 month period. Provided, however, that this section shall not excuse these individuals from any filing obligations imposed by a conflict of interest code adopted pursuant to Government Code Sections 87300, q. Regulation 18724 is applicable to judges who are assigned to hear cases on behalf of the County in its duty to provide judges to hear such cases. Judges who hear cases under a private agreement to which the County is not a party are not covered by Regulation 18724.
sections.question_synthetic: Are pro tem judges required to file statements of economic interests (Form 721) under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Pro tem judges are not required to file Form 721 if they serve less than 30 days in any 12-month period, unless a local conflict of interest code imposes filing obligations. The exemption applies only to judges assigned by the County; those serving under private agreement are not covered by the exemption.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone assistance. Document clearly presents question and conclusion. Minor uncertainty in full verbatim extraction due to document formatting, but key content accurately captured.

CONCLUSION: Regulation 18724 provides: Court commissioners, pro tern or retired judges shall not file statements of economic interests pursuant to Government Code Sections 87200, seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than 30 days in any 12 month period. Provided, however, that this section shall not excuse these individuals from any filing obligations imposed by a conflict of interest code adopted pursuant to Government Code Sections 87300, q. Regulation 18724 is applicable to judges who are assigned to hear cases on behalf of the County in its duty to provide judges to hear such cases. Judges who hear cases under a private agreement to which the County is not a party are not covered by Regulation 18724.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding whether pro tem judges must file statements of economic interests under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC clarifies that pro tem judges serving less than 30 days per year are exempt from filing requirements unless local conflict of interest codes impose obligations, with applicability limited to County-assigned judges.
